summary: Terrapin jam dominates a high‑energy second set with stellar Dark Star
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster Terrapin Station > The Wheel > Dark Star sequence, backed by Vince’s synth fireworks and tight dual drumming. Way To Go Home opens the set with punch, and the encore delivers Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds. The first set is slower but the overall performance is praised.
review: June 23, 1993 at Deer Creek was a night where the second‑set chemistry lit up the prairie. After a modest opening set, the band launched into Samson & Delilah and a soaring Way To Go Home before the legendary Terrapin Station jam unfolded. Jerry Garcia shredded through the Terrapin > The Wheel segue, his lead work described as “on fire” and blistering, while Vince Welnick’s synth textures added a fiery edge that many reviewers called perfect for this era. The dual drums of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann provided a chunky backbone that carried the post‑Terrapin space into an unforgettable Dark Star, later morphing into The Wheel and closing with Good Lovin'. The encore’s Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds capped a show remembered for its high‑energy improvisations despite a slower first half. Across all sources, the performance is hailed as one of the best mid‑’90s second‑set outings.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five recordings are explicitly positive, praising the Terrapin jam, Dark Star and overall energy; one mixed review notes occasional set‑flow issues, while a single negative provides no performance criticism. The strong consensus of praise yields a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard mix that captures the legendary Terrapin Station jam and post‑Terrapin improvisations with good balance and minimal drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Monster Terrapin Station > The Wheel > Dark Star sequence defines the second set.
Way To Go Home opens the set with punch, leading into a high‑energy flow.
Vince Welnick’s synth fireworks and tight dual drumming give the jams extra power.
song_highlights: Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds

band_performance.Jerry: Shredding, blistering lead work throughout the night, especially on Terrapin and Dark Star; relaxed yet confident solo on Way To Go Home.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a tight, locked‑in bass foundation that underpins the expansive improvisations.
band_performance.Vince: Fiery synth work on Terrapin and Easy Answers; midi effects praised as perfect for the era's sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums receive high praise for delivering a chunky rhythmic backbone during the post‑Terrapin space and jam sections.
band_performance.Billy: Strong dual drumming with Mickey adds drive and intensity, especially in the Terrapin > Drums > Space > Dark Star flow.

must_listen_sequences: Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds
